channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SONA 2026: Priority Legislation Doesn't Guarantee Law Passage
item.description: The article examines how presidents' priorities mentioned during State of the Nation Addresses (SONAs) often fail to become law, despite being labeled as 'priority legislation.' It highlights that ove...

item.title: Ontario arrests 63 in major internet child exploitation operation
item.description: Law enforcement in Ontario has made significant progress in combating online child exploitation, with 63 individuals arrested as part of a joint operation involving the Ontario Provincial Police (OPP)...

item.title: Macron's Courteous Gesture at Gabon's State Reception
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron and his wife welcomed Gabon's President Ali Bongo Ondimba and his wife, Marie Chantal Bongo, at a formal state reception at the Élysée Palace in Paris. The event, captured on...

item.title: Mississippi Congressman Calls for FBI Involvement in Mysterious Teen's Death
item.description: Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-MS), Mississippi's sole Black U.S. congressman, has called for federal intervention in the investigation of 18-year-old Nolan Wells' death after an independent autopsy failed t...

item.title: Experts Doubt Distillation Alone Explained Kimi K3's Success, Despite U.S. Allegations
item.description: The article discusses allegations by U.S. officials that Moonshot, the Chinese company behind Kimi K3, may have copied Anthropic’s Fable LLM to develop its model. White House science advisor Michael K...

item.title: Kenya's Mt Kenya by-election results signal political shift ahead of 2027 polls
item.description: Sammy Kamau, a young candidate from the Democracy for Citizens Party (DCP), won the Ol Kalou MP seat in Kenya's Mt Kenya region with a landslide victory, defeating the ruling United Democratic Allianc...

item.title: FG Allocates N500m for Strategic Communications Amid Genocide Allegations
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria spent N500 million in 2025 on an emergency strategic communications campaign to counter allegations of coordinated Western propaganda accusing the country of persecut...

item.title: Final Year Brethren Celebrations: Balancing Tradition and Financial Strain in Nigerian Universities
item.description: The article explores the evolution of 'Final Year Brethren' (FYB) celebrations in Nigerian universities, highlighting how these events have transformed from simple sign-out ceremonies into elaborate, ...

item.title: Chad Hugo Collaborates with Tierra Whack, Leikeli47, and Rochelle Jordan on New Single ‘JUMPUPW!NYA’

item.title: UK airport drop-off charges rise by average of £1.70, sparking debate over pricing and alternatives
item.description: Analysis reveals that drop-off fees at UK's major airports have increased by an average of £1.70 since last year, with some locations charging up to £10 for short periods. Gatwick Airport imposes a £1...

item.description: The article titled 'To kneel or not to kneel?' published on July 23, 2026, serves as a compilation of various news updates from Tanzania. It highlights several important developments across different ...

item.title: Kirinyaga Man's Journey from Shoe Sales to US Army Service
item.description: Dominic Kaiguri, a Kirinyaga native, has had an extraordinary journey from selling shoes online to becoming a high-speed soldier in the US Army. After leaving a television internship in 2022, he took ...

item.title: Motlanthe criticises governance failures and xenophobic violence in South Africa
item.description: Former President Kgalema Motlanthe has warned that xenophobic violence and social unrest in South Africa are driven by poor leadership and lack of sustained economic growth. Speaking at the Motlanthe ...

item.title: Lahore faces severe monsoon flooding and infrastructure challenges
item.description: Heavy monsoon rains caused widespread water accumulation across Lahore, severely disrupting daily life and traffic. Over 343 millimetres of rain fell in 48 hours, leading to stagnant water on major ro...

item.description: The Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) has formally requested the Office of the Ombudsman to reconsider its dismissal of graft complaints against former Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA)...

item.title: Bandits Kidnap 40 in Zamfara, Displace Thousands
item.description: Bandits have kidnapped at least 40 people and attacked communities in Zamfara State, forcing residents to flee their homes. The attacks, which lasted four consecutive days, have left several villages ...

item.title: Dawid Kruiper Municipality Faces Water Crisis and Corruption Probe
item.description: The Dawid Kruiper Local Municipality in South Africa is grappling with severe water infrastructure failures, including persistent leaks and sewage spills that have become a daily reality for residents...
